title: Mortgage Calculator for Virginia Residents: 2026 Guide
metaDescription: Free mortgage calculator for Virginia residents. Old Dominion property tax rates, county-by-county breakdowns, and Virginia Housing (VHDA) program details for 2026 homebuyers.

content.introduction:
Buying a home in Virginia — the Old Dominion — means navigating a housing market where the median price sits at $380,000 and effective property tax rates average 0.8%. A generic mortgage calculator won't cut it: you need one that factors in Virginia-specific variables like relatively low property taxes, county-level rate variations, and programs like Virginia Housing (VHDA).

This guide walks Virginia residents through exactly how to use a mortgage calculator for virginia residents to get accurate monthly payment estimates, understand county-by-county cost differences, and take advantage of state-specific homebuyer programs. Whether you're eyeing a home in Fairfax County or Henrico (Richmond) County, we'll show you how the numbers actually break down.

sections.heading: Why Virginia Homebuyers Need a State-Specific Calculator
sections.content:
National mortgage calculators use broad averages that miss critical Virginia details. Here's what makes the Old Dominion different:

  • Property Tax Range: Virginia rates span from 0.81% to 1.08% depending on county — a difference of $149/month on a median-priced home
  • Transfer Tax: Virginia charges a 0.25% transfer tax at closing, adding $950 on a median home
  • Closing Costs: Average 2.5% of loan amount in Virginia, or roughly $7,600 on a $304,000 loan
  • Conforming Loan Limit: $1,149,825 — homes above this need jumbo loans with different rates
  • Homestead Exemption: No general homestead exemption; localities may offer exemptions for 65+/disabled

Using a calculator that accounts for these Virginia-specific variables means your monthly payment estimate could differ by $101 or more compared to national averages.

sections.heading: Virginia Property Tax Rates by County
sections.content:
Property taxes are the single biggest variable in your mortgage payment beyond principal and interest. Here's how Virginia's top counties compare:

CountyMedian PriceTax RateEst. Payment
Fairfax$660,0001.03%$4,036/mo
Loudoun$620,0000.98%$3,764/mo
Prince William$470,0001.08%$2,893/mo
Virginia Beach$330,0000.82%$1,960/mo
Henrico (Richmond)$340,0000.81%$2,017/mo

The spread matters: a homebuyer in Fairfax County pays $4,036/month while the same purchase in Henrico (Richmond) County runs $2,017/month — a $2,019 monthly difference. Our mortgage calculator lets you input your exact county tax rate for precise estimates.

sections.heading: Virginia Housing (VHDA): First-Time Buyer Assistance
sections.content:
Virginia offers meaningful assistance through Virginia Housing (VHDA): Below-market rates + up to 2-2.5% of purchase price in down payment assistance; income limits vary by locality.

When using a mortgage calculator for Virginia, factor in how down payment assistance changes your numbers:

  • Without assistance: 20% down on a $380,000 home = $76,000 out of pocket
  • With Virginia Housing (VHDA): Potentially reduce your upfront cash by thousands, though this may increase your monthly payment
  • PMI consideration: Putting less than 20% down triggers PMI at roughly 0.5-1% of the loan annually ($127-$253/month)

Run both scenarios through the calculator to see which path saves more over your expected ownership period. For most Virginia buyers planning to stay 7+ years, the lower down payment with invested savings often wins.

sections.heading: How to Use a Mortgage Calculator for Virginia Properties
sections.content:
Follow this step-by-step process to get the most accurate estimate for your Virginia home purchase:

  1. Enter the purchase price for your target area (use county median prices above as a starting point)
  2. Set your down payment — 20% eliminates PMI; less requires PMI until you reach 20% equity
  3. Use the current rate — as of 2026, 30-year fixed rates hover near 6.875% (your rate depends on credit score and lender)
  4. Input your county's property tax rate — this is where most generic calculators fail Virginia residents
  5. Add insurance — homeowner's insurance in Virginia averages $1,520/year ($127/month)
  6. Include HOA if applicable — common in planned communities and condos

For a $380,000 home with 20% down at 6.875%, your estimated payment breaks down to:

  • Principal & Interest: $1,997
  • Property Tax: $253
  • Insurance: $127
  • Total: $2,377/month

sections.heading: 15-Year vs 30-Year Mortgage: Which Saves More?
sections.content:
On a $304,000 loan in Virginia:

TermRateMonthly P&ITotal Interest
30-year fixed6.875%$1,997$414,920
15-year fixed6.25%$2,607$165,260

The 15-year option saves $249,660 in interest but costs $610 more per month. For Virginia buyers, the 30-year term is often the pragmatic choice: keep the lower payment, invest the difference, and make extra principal payments when cash flow allows.

Use our mortgage calculator to model extra payment scenarios — adding even $200/month to a 30-year mortgage can cut years off the term.

sections.heading: Virginia Closing Cost Breakdown
sections.content:
Beyond your monthly payment, Virginia homebuyers should budget for these one-time closing costs:

  • Origination fees: 0.5-1% of loan ($2,280 on median)
  • Title insurance: $1,900 average in Virginia
  • Appraisal: $400-$600
  • Transfer tax: 0.25% = $950
  • Recording fees: $50-$250
  • Prepaid taxes & insurance: 2-6 months escrowed

Total estimated closing costs: $7,600 (2.5% of loan). Combined with your down payment, expect to bring $83,600 to close on a median-priced Virginia home.

sections.heading: Virginia Housing Market Context for 2026
sections.content:
Virginia's housing market in 2026 reflects broader trends shaped by the state's near-average cost of living (COL index: 104, where 100 = national average). With a median household income of $87,249, the typical Virginia household dedicates roughly 33% of gross income to a median-priced home payment — above the recommended 28% threshold.

Key market factors for Virginia:

  • Income tax impact: 2% to 5.75% across 4 brackets; 5.75% applies above $17,001 — effectively flat for most filers — factor this into your affordability calculation
  • Conforming loan limit: $1,149,825 — median home falls well within this limit
  • Insurance landscape: Virginia homeowner's insurance reflects regional risks like hurricanes and flooding

content.practicalExample:
Real-World Example: Fairfax County Homebuyer

Buyer Profile: A couple with a combined income of $87,249 looking to buy in Fairfax County, Virginia.

Property: $660,000 home | 20% down ($132,000) | 6.875% 30-year fixed

Monthly Breakdown:

  • Principal & Interest: $3,469
  • Property Tax (1.03%): $567
  • Insurance: $220
  • Total: $4,256/month

This represents 59% of their gross monthly income. This exceeds the 28% guideline — they may want to consider a lower price point or larger down payment.

Try running your own Virginia scenarios with our mortgage calculator.
content.conclusion:
A mortgage calculator built for Virginia residents accounts for the Old Dominion's specific property tax structure (0.8% average), closing cost norms (2.5%), and programs like Virginia Housing (VHDA) that can significantly change your numbers. The county-level differences across Virginia — from Fairfax to Henrico (Richmond) — mean that where you buy matters as much as what you buy.

Use our free mortgage calculator to run scenarios with your actual county tax rate, compare 15 vs 30-year terms, and see how extra payments accelerate your payoff. The math is straightforward once you have the right inputs — and now you know exactly which inputs Virginia requires.

faqs.question: What is the average property tax rate in Virginia?
faqs.answer: Virginia's effective property tax rate averages 0.8%, but varies significantly by county. Fairfax County has a 1.03% rate while Henrico (Richmond) County sits at 0.81%. On a $380,000 home, the state average translates to $3,040/year or $253/month added to your mortgage payment. Always use your specific county rate when running mortgage calculations.

faqs.question: Does Virginia have first-time homebuyer programs?
faqs.answer: Yes. Virginia's primary program is Virginia Housing (VHDA): Below-market rates + up to 2-2.5% of purchase price in down payment assistance; income limits vary by locality. Additionally, the homestead exemption provides: No general homestead exemption; localities may offer exemptions for 65+/disabled. Check eligibility requirements as income limits and other conditions apply.

faqs.question: How much house can I afford in Virginia on a $87000 salary?
faqs.answer: Using the 28% rule, a $87,249 gross income allows roughly $2,036/month for housing. At 6.875% with 20% down and 0.8% property tax, this supports approximately a $276,663 home. Run exact numbers using our mortgage calculator with your specific income, debts, and target county.

faqs.question: What are closing costs in Virginia?
faqs.answer: Virginia closing costs average 2.5% of the loan amount, or about $7,600 on a $304,000 mortgage. This includes lender fees, title insurance, appraisal, state transfer tax (0.25%), and prepaid taxes/insurance. Combined with a 20% down payment, budget $83,600 total cash to close.
